content.text: I'll start by inspecting the cluster state.

input.command:
NS=karma-rabbitmq-full-lifecycle-01-2026-7025b4c1-cluster-a
kubectl -n $NS get pods
echo "---SVC---"
kubectl -n $NS get svc
echo "---VHOSTS---"
kubectl -n $NS exec rabbitmq-0 -- rabbitmqctl list_vhosts
echo "---POLICIES---"
kubectl -n $NS exec rabbitmq-0 -- rabbitmqctl list_policies -p /app
echo "---QUEUES---"
kubectl -n $NS exec rabbitmq-0 -- rabbitmqctl list_queues -p /app name type policy

content.text: 3 nodes healthy, RabbitMQ 3.12. Now I'll set the ha-all policy for classic queue mirroring on /app.

input.command:
NS=karma-rabbitmq-full-lifecycle-01-2026-7025b4c1-cluster-a
kubectl -n $NS exec rabbitmq-0 -- rabbitmqctl set_policy -p /app ha-all "^app-queue$" '{"ha-mode":"all","ha-sync-mode":"automatic"}' --apply-to queues

content.content: Setting policy "ha-all" for pattern "^app-queue$" to "{"ha-mode":"all","ha-sync-mode":"automatic"}" with priority "0" for vhost "/app" ...

tool_use_result.stdout: Setting policy "ha-all" for pattern "^app-queue$" to "{"ha-mode":"all","ha-sync-mode":"automatic"}" with priority "0" for vhost "/app" ...
